title: aulomyrcia exsucca
intro: Aulomyrcia exsucca, also known as the "dead myrtle," is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to Brazil and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Aulomyrcia exsucca include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Aulomyrcia exsucca can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
